coredata.dc:title: Single-atom Cu-attached MOFs as peroxide-like enzymes to construct dual-mode immunosensors for detection of breast cancer typing in serum
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Ning
Zhang, Ya
Xu, Ying
Liu, Xiaofang
Chen, Jian
Yang, Mei
Hou, Changjun
Huo, Danqun
coredata.dc:description: Molecular subtype of breast cancer has defined the treatment direction of breast cancer and guided the selection of drugs. Invasive tests such as tissue biopsy have the potential to promote the metastasis of cancer cells. Therefore, the continuous development of high-performance and low-cost in vitro diagnostic tools is crucial for the diagnosis and treatment of cancer prognosis. Liquid biopsy techniques provide noninvasive, real-time, dynamic, multicomponent, quantitative, and long-term observations at the cellular, genetic, and molecular levels in vivo. A Cu-Zr metal-organic framework(MOF) nanomases with monatomic Cu attachment has been synthesized by covalently attaching single atom Cu to the defect site of zirconia clusters in the UiO-66. The catalytic performance of Cu/UiO-66 as a peroxide-like enzyme was verified by enzyme kinetics calculation. Using o-phenylenediamine as substrate, a colorimetric fluorescence dual-mode immunosensor was constructed by introducing blue fluorescent carbon quantum dots (CDs) based on ultraviolet chromogenic reaction and fluorescence internal filtration effect. By magnetic adsorption separation, the background signal of the sensor is reduced and the sensitivity of the sensor is further improved. In 0.001–1 ng/mL detection range, detection of HER2/ER/PR/Ki-67, the result has obvious linear relationship. Among them, the colorimetric detection limit were 0.367/0.379/0.392/0.388 pg/mL; the ratio fluorescence detection limit can reach 0.3, 0.289, 0.257, 0.33 pg/mL, with high sensitivity. The proteins HER2, ER, PR and Ki-67 in serum related to human breast cancer typing were detected with high sensitivity. Finally, the actual serum samples were tested using sensors. According to the confirmed detection criteria, the classification of serum breast cancer was preliminarily explored with an accuracy of 87.5 %. The sensor has high catalytic performance and excellent real-time detection performance of biomarkers, and have practical application potential in human serum level detection. It provides a new method for the classification of breast cancer in serum.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Monatomic catalysis Cu-Zr MOF
Nanase
Peroxidase activity
CDs；Colorimetric and fluorescence two-mode detection
Liquid biopsy techniques
Breast cancer typing in serum

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: HER2, ER, PR, Ki-67 colorimetric method and ratio fluorescence detection
Selectivity and reproducibility of dual-mode immune sensors
Detection of HER2, ER, PR, Ki-67 and breast cancer typing in real samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM spectra of (A) UiO-66 and (F) Cu/UiO-66. (B-E) UiO-66 corresponding EDS spectrum. (G-J) Cu/UiO-66 corresponding EDS spectrum.
(A) Varying the OPD concentration at a fixed 18 mM H2O2. (B) Tuning the H2O2 concentration at a fixed 11.1 mM OPD. (C-D) Double-reciprocal plots of parts A and B, respectively.
Feasibility detection of multicolorimetric (A) and ratiometric fluorescent (B) dual-mode immunosensors. (C) Coincidence plot of the fluorescence emission of CDs with the DAP absorption spectrum.

(A) UV spectrum at different concentrations of HER2 and (B) corresponding linear fitting diagram. (C) Fluorescence spectra at different concentrations of HER2 and (D) corresponding linear fitting diagram.

(A–F) UV spectrum at different concentrations of ER, PR, Ki-67 and corresponding linear fitting diagram. (H-M) Fluorescence spectra at different concentrations of ER, PR, Ki-67 and corresponding linear fitting diagram.
Detection results of real serum samples and molecular typing of breast cancer.
Schematic of a dual-mode immunosensor for HER2 detection: colorimetric and ratiometric fluorescence.

head.ce:title: Single-atom Cu-attached MOFs as peroxide-like enzymes to construct dual-mode immunosensors for detection of breast cancer typing in serum

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory for Biorheological Science and Technology of Ministry of Education, Bioengineering College of Chongqing University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Molecular subtype of breast cancer has defined the treatment direction of breast cancer and guided the selection of drugs. Invasive tests such as tissue biopsy have the potential to promote the metastasis of cancer cells. Therefore, the continuous development of high-performance and low-cost in vitro diagnostic tools is crucial for the diagnosis and treatment of cancer prognosis. Liquid biopsy techniques provide noninvasive, real-time, dynamic, multicomponent, quantitative, and long-term observations at the cellular, genetic, and molecular levels in vivo. A Cu-Zr metal-organic framework(MOF) nanomases with monatomic Cu attachment has been synthesized by covalently attaching single atom Cu to the defect site of zirconia clusters in the UiO-66. The catalytic performance of Cu/UiO-66 as a peroxide-like enzyme was verified by enzyme kinetics calculation. Using o-phenylenediamine as substrate, a colorimetric fluorescence dual-mode immunosensor was constructed by introducing blue fluorescent carbon quantum dots (CDs) based on ultraviolet chromogenic reaction and fluorescence internal filtration effect. By magnetic adsorption separation, the background signal of the sensor is reduced and the sensitivity of the sensor is further improved. In 0.001–1 ng/mL detection range, detection of HER2/ER/PR/Ki-67, the result has obvious linear relationship. Among them, the colorimetric detection limit were 0.367/0.379/0.392/0.388 pg/mL; the ratio fluorescence detection limit can reach 0.3, 0.289, 0.257, 0.33 pg/mL, with high sensitivity. The proteins HER2, ER, PR and Ki-67 in serum related to human breast cancer typing were detected with high sensitivity. Finally, the actual serum samples were tested using sensors. According to the confirmed detection criteria, the classification of serum breast cancer was preliminarily explored with an accuracy of 87.5 %. The sensor has high catalytic performance and excellent real-time detection performance of biomarkers, and have practical application potential in human serum level detection. It provides a new method for the classification of breast cancer in serum.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Breast cancer is a kind of malignant tumor with the highest incidence in world women. It is also a major threat to women's health and life safety [1]. According to the 2023 Cancer Report, breast cancer remains the most prevalent cancer in women, with 297,790 new cancer cases, accounting for 31 % of female cancers. The death toll stands at 43,000 [2]. The diagnosis and treatment technology of breast cancer is developing rapidly, but treatment failure and tumor recurrence are still the main causes of death of breast cancer patients [3]. Studies have found that for breast cancer patients of the same stage or the same pathological type, there are significant differences in their survival and sensitivity to radiotherapy and chemotherapy, mainly because breast cancer is a type of highly heterogeneous tumor, different subtypes have varying degrees of influence on its clinical diagnosis, choice of the treatment plan, evaluation of drug efficacy and prognosis judgment [4–6]. The expression levels of estrogen receptor (ER), progesterone receptor (PR), ERBB2 receptor (HER2) and a protein related to nuclear division and proliferation (Ki-67) are closely related to tumor growth invasion, metastasis and recurrence [7–10]. Therefore, there is an urgent need to develop highly sensitive tumor heterogeneity detection technology to achieve individualized treatment of breast cancer.

Traditionally, molecular typing methods for breast cancer based on detection methods such as gene chips, immunohistochemistry, and genomics all have obvious shortcomings. They can only conduct qualitative and semi-quantitative research on some key molecules in the development of tumors [11–13]. The interrelationship between key molecules and clinical information cannot be displayed in real-time, so it is difficult to accurately reveal the process of tumor progression and comprehensively evaluate tumor invasion behavior on the macroscopic morphology [14–16]. More importantly, these technologies need to extract and process tumor cells or tissue specimens from the body, which loses the three-dimensional information of the primary tumor, and invasive detection methods may lead to tumor metastasis [13]. Compared with traditional gene chip and immunohistochemistry technology, liquid biopsy technology can be noninvasive, real-time, dynamic, multi-component, quantitative, and long-term observation at the cellular, genetic and molecular level in a living state, reflecting breast cancer. This is of great significance for screening the advantageous populations of targeted therapy based on the expression of specific tumor biomarkers, optimizing clinical targeted drug treatment plans, evaluating the efficacy of molecular targeting, and making prognostic judgments at the molecular level [17–19].

The advantage of immune sensors is that the binding of antigens to antibodies is highly specific, thus reducing non-specific interference [20,21]. However, they do have certain limitations. For example, some immune sensors often have high background interference, poor stability and reproducibility, and few immune sensors are widely used in practical applications due to issues such as cost, availability, and speed of analysis [22]. However, in recent years, with the continuous development of nanomaterials, some new nanomaterials, such as carbon nanotubes, graphene, Carbon quantum dots, MOFs, etc., can be used as signals and substrate catalysts to improve sensitivity and accuracy in the development of nanoimmune sensors [23,24]. The new immunosensors, such as colorimetric, fluorescent, surface-enhanced Raman scattering, electrochemical and piezoelectric, have the advantages of higher sensitivity, convenience, simplicity and wide linear range, and can detect various biomarkers well [20,21,25].

Nowadays, more and more protein biomarkers have been detected. Currently, most of the widely used kits are enzyme-linked immunity (ELISA), in which catalase is a natural enzyme with highly specific and catalytic properties [26,27]. They are an extremely important biocatalysts and play an important role in many biological reactions. However, they have certain limitations, such as poor stability (variability, hydrolyzability), intolerance to harsh reaction conditions, difficulty in purification and poor reusability, which greatly affect their wide biological applications [28,29]. To overcome this limitation of natural enzymes, nanomases have been developed through continuous exploration and research over the past decades, allowing them to both have the unique properties of nanomaterials and mimic enzymes with catalytic functions[30]. Nano-enzymes have the characteristics of high stability, adjustable enzyme activity, diverse structure and extensibility of preparation. Compared with natural enzymes, nano-enzymes have been widely used in medicine, chemical industry, food, agriculture, environment and other fields [31–33]. Due to the regular arrangement of metal nodes and organic ligands in the crystal structure, MOFs have been widely used in recent years as precursors for the preparation of many MOF derivatives, such as nanoporous carbon, nanomases of metallic materials, peroxidase like nanomases and metal oxide nanomases [34–36]. These derivatives have excellent characteristics of parent MOFs, such as large surface area, high porosity, and easy chemical modification. Therefore, MOFs are ideal materials for constructing nano-enzymes [37,38] Metal cations such as Au+, Ag+, Fe3+, Co2+, etc. exhibit various enzyme activities under different conditions, and the metal centers with residual valence on the back surface of the crystal forming the metal node with MOF are regarded as the active centers of enzymatic reactions. Under different conditions, the activities of oxidase, peroxidase, catalase and superoxide dismutase can be shown and have been extensively studied in catalysis, sensing, detection and imaging [39,40].

Here, we construct a monatomic catalyst by covalently attaching monatomic Cu to defect sites of zirconia clusters in the UiO-66. Specific MOFs can not only stabilize these atoms to maintain large specific surface area and other properties, but also have good oxidase activity, which makes them more widely used in biosensors. The Scheme 1 describes the principle of a dual-mode colorimetric and ratio fluorescence immunosensor platform based on uniatomic Cu-attached UiO-66. The Scheme 1 A and Scheme 1 B show the synthesis process of the material and the modification of the antibody by glutaraldehyde crosslinking, respectively. The Scheme 1C shows the detection process of the sensor platform. Firstly, a certain amount of antibody-modified Fe3O4 NPs and different concentrations of HER2 antigen were incubated, and then Cu/UiO-66 (Cu/UiO-66-Ab) modified with secondary antibody was added. The typical "sandwich" structure was formed by modifying Fe3O4 NPs, antigen, and Cu/UiO-66-Ab with antibody. The background signal of the sensor was reduced by magnetic bead adsorption, and the Cu/UiO-66 concentration corresponding to the target protein concentration was obtained. Based on the peroxidase activity of Cu/UiO-66, in the presence of H2O2, the color developing substrate o-phenylenediamine (OPD) was catalyzed to produce yellow product 2, 3-diaminophenazine (DAP). Different antigen concentrations showed different shades of color effect, and showed different absorption peaks at 425 nm in the ultraviolet absorption spectrum. Similarly, the ratio fluorescence detection is based on the internal filtering effect (IFE) between the generated 2, 3-diaminophenazine, which exhibits obvious yellow fluorescence at 556 nm, and the CDs, which produces blue fluorescence at 424 nm, which effectively quenishes the fluorescence at 424 nm to form a ratio fluorescence signal. Therefore, through the independent response and mutual confirmation of the fluorescence immunosensors by colorimetric method and ratio method, HER2 detection performance was finally demonstrated.

Fig. 1A and F show SEM images of regular octahedral UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66 coated with copper [41]. The synthesized UiO-66 has regular octahedral structure, and a thin Cu-O film is generated when copper is coated and grown on its surface. Therefore, the size of Cu/UiO-66 is larger than that of UiO-66, and the edges of the regular octahedron gradually become blunt and spherical [42]. Fig. 1B–E shows the EDS spectrum of UiO-66, whose elements mainly contain C, O, and Zr, while the EDS spectrum of Cu/UiO-66. In addition to the main elements contained in UiO-66, the EDS spectrum of Cu/UiO-66 also contains Cu elements distributed on its surface, indicating that Cu/ UiO-66 has a thin Cu film.

The chemical structure and composition of UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66 have been studied by FT-IR. As shown in Fig. S1A, the peak value at 3345 cm-1 is the tensile vibration peak of O-H of crystalline water and physically adsorbed water condensed in the cavity [43]. The peaks at 1579 cm-1, 1400 cm-1 and 745 cm-1 represent the tensile vibration peaks of CO, CC and Zr-O of UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66, respectively, indicating that UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66 are the same Zr-based MOF materials [42]. Among them, the most obvious difference is that Cu/UiO-66 has a Cu-O tensile vibration peak at 1665 cm-1, which is formed by covalent bond between copper atoms and UiO-66 defect center. In Fig. S1B, the amino stretching vibration peak of APTES modified Cu/UiO-66 appears at 3411 cm-1, indicating that the amino group on the surface of Cu/UiO-66 has been successfully modified. The X-ray diffraction pattern in Fig. S1C further illustrates that the crystal structure of UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66 is roughly the same. It can be seen from the figure that the main crystal plane (110) and (002) of UiO-66 are reflected in the same diffraction peak position of 7.59° and 8.72° of Cu/UiO-66 [44]. In addition, other crystal plane diffraction peaks, such as (022), (113), (004), (115), (224) and (046) of UiO-66 correspond to each other in Cu/UiO-66, indicating that the Cu layer only covers the surface of UiO-66 and does not break the crystal structure of UiO-66 [42]. Fig. S1D shows the detection of ultraviolet absorption spectra of Cu/UiO-66 surface modified aptamer by glutaraldehyde crosslinking method. The protein absorption peak at 280 nm shows the successful modification of the antibody.

In the full-scan XPS maps of UiO-66 and Cu/UiO-66, as shown in Fig. S2A, diffraction peaks were observed at 932 eV, 531 eV, 284 eV, and 183 eV representing Cu2p, C1s, O1s, and Zr3d, respectively. Similarly, the difference from UiO-66 is the Cu2p diffraction peak in Fig. S2B, which appears at 932.5 eV and 952.3 eV representing Cu2p3/2 and Cu2p1/2, respectively [42,45]. The main peak at 932.5 eV indicates that Cu is mainly attached to the surface of UiO-66 as a single atom in zero valence state.

As shown in Fig. S3A, using o-phenylenediamine (OPD) as the color developing substrate, OPD (colorless) can be catalyzed by Cu/UiO-66 to become DAP (yellow), and the UV absorption peak of the reaction system at 425 nm changes. Using TMB as the color development substrate, according to the color development results in Fig. S3B, only in the presence of Cu/UiO-66, TMB and H2O2, Cu/UiO-66 catalyzed TMB to form ox-TMB, which showed obvious blue color, indicating that it had a peroxidase simulation activity.

In order to further analyze its catalytic activity, different concentrations of OPD and H2O2 were used as the kinetic parameters of the substrate under the condition of the same concentration of Cu/UiO-66. Fig. 2 shows that the catalytic behavior of Cu/UiO-66 is consistent with the Michaelis-Menton model. The Vmax and Km with OPD and H2O2 as substrates were calculated using the double reciprocal diagram. The Km value of Cu/UiO-66 with OPD as substrate was 0.35 mM, indicating that Cu/UiO-66 had a high affinity with OPD. The kinetic parameters of Cu/UiO-66 were obtained by recording the ultraviolet-visible absorption intensity of the mixture at 425 nm over time. The calculation of kinetic constants is based on the double reciprocal graph of Michaelis-Menten equation.
1 V
=
(
1
[ S ]
)
(
K
m
V
max
)
+
1
V
max
Where, V is the initial velocity, [S] is the concentration of OPD or H2O2, Vmax and Km are the maximum reaction velocity and Michaelis constant, which can reflect the affinity between the substrate and the simulated enzyme [46].

The Km value of Cu/ UiO-66 using H2O2 as substrate is 3.54 mM, slightly lower than the Km value of horseradish peroxidase (HRP) reported in the literature (3.70 mM) [47]. The results show that Cu/UiO-66, as a novel catalase-like enzyme, has high catalytic activity and can be widely used in the construction of nano-enzyme biosensors.

As shown in Fig. S4A, the monodisperse quantum dots show uniform shapes in TEM images, and their average particle size is 4.4 nm (Fig. S4B). CDs shows a maximum emission wavelength of 450 nm at an excitation wavelength of 370 nm (Fig. S5C). In view of this, we successfully prepared CDs.

We investigated the feasibility of the designed colorimetric and ratio fluorescence dual-mode immunosensors. Fig. 3A shows colorimetric detection. OPD (red line), OPD and Cu/UiO-66 (pink line) do not have obvious UV absorption peaks at 425 nm. When H2O2 is added (blue line), the UV-Vis spectrum produces obvious absorption peaks at 425 nm. After binding with the 1 ng/mL target HER2, the UV absorption peak at 425 nm decreased significantly. The results showed that the amount of Cu/UiO-66-Ab decreased significantly after adsorption by magnetic beads. As shown in Fig. 3B, when Cu/UiO-66 does not exist, when CDs, CDs+OPD, CDs+OPD+H2O2 are added, their fluorescence values do not change significantly at 424 nm, indicating that the fluorescence of CDs is not interfered by other substances. In the presence of Cu/UiO-66 (CDs+OPD+ Cu/UiO-66 +H2O2), the fluorescence peak emission of CDs at 424 nm decreased significantly, while the emission peak appeared at 556 nm. Similarly, after the addition of 1 ng/mL target HER2 protein, the DAP fluorescence at 556 nm decreased significantly after oxidation. Due to the internal filtration effect, the fluorescence of CDs decreased due to quenching ability, and the blue fluorescence of quantum dots at 424 nm was significantly enhanced. Therefore, the follow-up experiment can detect the four marker proteins by colorimetric and fluorescence modes. We further verified the internal filtering effect between CDs and DAP, as shown in Fig. 3C. The absorption spectrum of DAP overlapped with the CDs emission spectrum, resulting in the quenching of the fluorescence of the CDs emission peak.

As shown in Fig. S5A, when the added CDs is less than 100 μL, the fluorescence ratio I556/I424 is greater than 1, indicating that DAP can achieve sufficient quenching of CDs, but the fluorescence value of I424 is too low to significantly show the fluorescence quenching effect at 424 nm. On the other hand, when the added CDs is greater than 100 μL, the fluorescence ratio I556/I424 is less than 1, indicating that the concentration of CDs is too high, and DAP cannot quench the fluorescence of CDs. Similarly, the fluorescence at 556 nm cannot be obviously increased with the increase of target concentration. Therefore, when CDs with volume of 100 μL is added, the fluorescence ratio I556/I424 is closer to 1, which can better display the change of fluorescence intensity and improve the sensitivity of the sensor. Secondly, Fig. S5B and Fig. S5C showed that the best detection results were obtained when the catalytic time of colorimetric and fluorescence detection was both 12 min, so 12 min was selected as the best catalytic reaction time.

ce:section.ce:section-title: HER2, ER, PR, Ki-67 colorimetric method and ratio fluorescence detection
ce:section.ce:para: Under optimal conditions, HER2 was detected by colorimetric and ratio fluorescence two-mode signals. Firstly, colorimetric detection was carried out by the absorption peak of the ultraviolet absorption spectrum of color change. As shown in Fig. 4A, with the increase of HER2 concentration (a-f: 1, 50, 250, 500, 750, 1000 pg/mL), the UV absorption peak of DAP at 425 nm gradually decreases and the color gradually becomes lighter. In Fig. 4B, the linear regression equation between the UV absorption peak and HER2 concentration can be expressed as YH1= −2.305 * 10-4X H1 + 0.3233 (R2 = 0.9912), and the calculated detection limit is 0.367 pg/mL (S/N = 3). Secondly, the ratio fluorescence detection was performed according to the change of fluorescence intensity. As shown in Fig. 4C, with the increasing concentration of HER2, the fluorescence peak of DAP at 556 nm gradually increased, and the fluorescence intensity of CDs at 424 nm gradually decreased due to IFE effect. In Fig. 4D, the linear relationship between the ratio fluorescence value of I556/I424 and HER2 concentration is YH2= −1.820 * 10-3XH2+ 2.634 (R2 = 0.9993) in the linear range of 1–1000 pg/mL, and the detection limit is 0.3 pg/mL (S/N = 3). These results indicate that the dual-mode immunoassay sensor platform constructed by us can achieve high sensitivity and reliable detection of HER2, and the immune sensor has ideal detection linear range and detection limits compared with other methods(Table S1).

Under the same conditions, colorimetric and rate-fluorescence dual-mode signals of the other three proteins associated with breast cancer typing, ER, PR and Ki-67 were detected. As shown in Fig. 5A–F, in the same linear range (1–1000 pg/mL), the UV absorption peak at 425 nm has obvious changes and good linear fitting relationship. The linear regression equation between the concentration of ER and the UV absorption peak was YE1= −2.401 * 10-4X + 0.3340 (R2 = 0.9806), and the estimated detection limit was 0.379 pg/mL (S/N = 3). The linear regression equation of PR concentration and UV absorption peak was YP1= −2.539 * 10-4X + 0.3450 (R2 = 0.9973), and the estimated detection limit was 0.392 pg/mL (S/N = 3). The linear regression equation of Ki-67 concentration and UV absorption peak was YK1= −2.656 * 10-4X + 0.3420 (R2 = 0.9961), and the detection limit was 0.388 pg/mL (S/N = 3). Similarly, in the detection of rate-fluorescence dual-mode signals, as shown in Fig. 5H–M, within the detection range of 1–1000 pg/mL, with 370 nm as the excitation peak, the measured emission peak ratio at 556 nm and 424 nm varied with the concentration of the target, and had a good linear fitting relationship. The linear regression equation of ER concentration and ratio fluorescence value I556/I424 was YE2= −1.510 * 10-3X + 2.544 (R2 = 0.9583), and the estimated detection limit was 0.289 pg/mL (S/N = 3). The linear regression equation of the concentration and ratio fluorescence value of PR I556/I424 was YP2 = −1.130 * 10-3X + 2.265 (R2 = 0.9741), and the estimated detection limit was 0.257 pg/mL (S/N = 3). The linear regression equation of Ki-67 concentration and ratio fluorescence value I556/I424 was YK1 = −1.730 * 10-3X + 2.901 (R2 = 0.9899), and the detection limit was 0.33 pg/mL (S/N = 3). The ratio fluorescence detection results and UV detection results have relatively low detection limits. The sensor has different signal response pathways, which are independent of each other in HER2 detection. Therefore, this two-mode detection platform can show more intuitive, accurate and sensitive performance.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and reproducibility of dual-mode immune sensors
ce:section.ce:para: When the four marker proteins were detected, the other three proteins, CEA and PSA were selected as interference proteins to verify the selectivity of the proposed scheme. As shown in Fig. S6A, the detection value ΔAbs=∣Given value - white value given∣that the colorimetric absorption peaks and fluorescence intensity of the experimental group (including 1 ng/mL target protein and 10 ng/mL interfering substances) are significantly lower than other interfering groups due to the specific recognition of antigen and antibody, indicating that the colorimetric mode of this sensor has excellent selectivity. In addition, the ratio fluorescence mode is also selected, see Fig. S6B, the detection valueΔI556/I424 = ∣given actual detection value- blank value given∣, the ratio fluorescence of the experimental group is significantly lower than other interference groups, indicating that the ratio fluorescence detection mode of this sensor is also very selective.

In addition, we examined the results of six parallel experiments with UV and fluorescence when the concentrations of HER2, ER, PR and Ki-67 were all 1 ng/mL, as shown in Fig. S6C -D. There was no significant difference in the detection of the four target proteins by colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode immune sensors, indicating that the biosensor had excellent reproducibility.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of HER2, ER, PR, Ki-67 and breast cancer typing in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: In order to further explore the practicability and reliability of the established dual-modal method in the detection of real samples, real samples (serum from breast cancer patients, Affiliated Cancer Hospital of Chongqing University) were diluted to 1 % for detection. Colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode detection were performed on serum of 3 healthy patients and 5 breast cancer patients respectively, and the detection results were arranged into a table, as shown in Fig. 6. Compared with the serum detection results of normal people, the concentration of positive expression of related proteins in breast cancer patients was significantly increased. The negative expression of related proteins was similar to that of normal people.

Further molecular typing of 5 breast cancer patients was performed according to the detection results. Among them, BC1 is "triple negative", BC2 is "Luminal B type (HER2 positive)", BC3 is "Luminal B type (HER2 positive)", BC4 is "Luminal A type" and BC5 is "Luminal B type (HER2 positive)". Compared with the typing results of clinical immunohistochemical method, the accuracy rate reached 87.5 %, which proved that the biosensor has a certain reliability and can be used for the detection of HER2 in actual samples.

In this paper, by the synthesis of MOF with peroxidase activity, a colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode immune sensor was constructed to realize the sensitive detection of HER2, ER, PR, Ki-67 and the classification of breast cancer. Cu with nano-enzyme activity was synthesized by attaching single atom Cu to the surface of UiO-66 by hydrothermal method. The simulated activity of peroxidase was investigated. The Km value of Cu/UiO-66 is 0.35 mM and the substrate affinity is obvious. The traditional enzyme (HRP) can be further developed in the field of sensing. Cu/UO-66 catalyzes the formation of 2, 3-diaminophenazine (DAP), a yellow product with yellow fluorescence, from o-phenylendiamine (OPD), which has an obvious UV absorption peak at 425 nm and fluorescence emission peak at 566 nm. Based on the principle of internal filtration effect (IFE), the fluorescence of DAP at 556 nm can quenched the CDs fluorescence at 424 nm obviously by adding CDs with blue fluorescence. Based on this, the target protein can be detected by colorimetric and ratio fluorescence modes. Magnetic bead adsorption separation method is used to reduce the background signal of sensor. Under optimal experimental conditions, the sensor achieves high sensitivity detection of four target proteins in the linear range of 1–1000 pg/mL. The sensor has a low detection limit and performs well in selectivity and reproducibility tests. In the detection of actual samples, the serological typing detection of breast cancer patients was realized through the specific detection results. Compared with the clinical typing results of the sample, the accuracy reached 87.5 %, thus providing a new idea for the realization of breast cancer typing detection in serum.

Ya Zhang conceived the idea, designed the study, performed experiments, analyzed the results, and wrote the manuscript. Xiaofang Liu,Ning Li,Ying Xu,Yi Ma，Zhen Huang, Huibo Luo conceived the idea and designed the study. Changjun Hou，Danqun Huo provided valuable suggestions and discussed the results.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (81772290), Fundamental Research Founds for the Central Universities (2022CDJYGRH-013)，Graduate Scientific Research and Innovation Foundation of Chongqing, China (CYB22073)，Sichuan Province Science and Technology Support Program (2022YFSY0013).

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.snb.2023.134903.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Xia, X., Zhang, J., Lu, N., 2015. Pd-Ir Core-Shell Nanocubes: A Type of Highly Efficient and Versatile Peroxidase Mimic. ACS Nano. 9 (10), 9994–10004.

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Ya Zhang is presently a Ph.D. student in College of Bioengineering at Chongqing University, People’s Republic of China. Her research interests include the development of new nanomaterials for application in optoelectronic devices and analysis.

Changjun Hou completed his Ph.D. in biomedical engineering in 2004 at Chongqing University. After a period of research at the University of Illinois at Urbana- Champaign as a visiting scholar, he returned to China and got tenure at the college of bioengineering in Chongqing University. In 2010, he became a full-professor in biomedical engineering. His scientific interests rest on the design and construction of biochemical sensing systems based on porphyrins and other sensitive materials.
